Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The Results state that Os10g0503300 was identified and subsequently named OsBEBT.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The title/abstract and Results explicitly define BEBT as benzoyl-CoA:benzyl alcohol benzoyltransferase.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Os10g0503300 is described as encoding a clade-V BAHD acyltransferase and containing a C-terminal type-1 peroxisomal targeting signal (PTS1).

Source_Species.reason: OsBEBT is a rice gene studied in this paper.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Rice is identified as Oryza sativa in the paper.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: The bebt mutants are stated to be in the japonica cultivar ZH11 background.

Applied_Species.reason: Core genetic tests for OsBEBT were performed in rice.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The applied species for the core in planta assays is Oryza sativa.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The bebt mutants analysed in Results are in ZH11.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: The paper identifies OsBEBT as part of the phenylalanine-derived salicylic acid biosynthetic pathway.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: OsBEBT is presented as part of the dedicated triple-enzyme terminal SA biosynthetic module after beta-oxidation.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Results place OsBEBT downstream of OsCNL/beta-oxidation-derived benzoyl-CoA and upstream of BBH/BSE through metabolite accumulation and rescue assays.

Primary_Substrate.reason: In vitro assays showed highest catalytic efficiency toward benzoyl-CoA when benzyl alcohol was the acyl acceptor.

Primary_Product.reason: The enzyme is shown to form benzylbenzoate in vitro and be required for benzylbenzoate accumulation in vivo.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: The Results explicitly state that OsBEBT transfers the benzoyl group from benzoyl-CoA to benzyl alcohol to form benzylbenzoate.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Mutant and complementation data place benzylbenzoate as a direct upstream intermediate in SA biosynthesis.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: The paper clearly shows OsBEBT is a key dedicated enzyme essential for SA biosynthesis, but it does not explicitly call it a bottleneck or rate-limiting/control step.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: YFP-OsBEBT co-localized with the peroxisome marker CFP-SKL in N. benthamiana.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: OsCNL1-based candidate selection notes that OsCNL1 is strongly upregulated by SA-inducing stimuli and that OsBEBT is highly co-expressed with OsCNL1; the paper also states the module shows pathogen-induced gene expression patterns.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper states the triple-enzyme module has pathogen-induced gene expression patterns and discusses responsiveness to SA-inducing stimuli.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: OsBEBT is linked directly to salicylic acid biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: SA is described as a plant hormone; chemically it is salicylic acid, a phenolic acid, consistent with the paper's framing.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The introduction states SA is central to plant immunity and also functions in abiotic stress response, development and root microbiome assembly.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The core metabolite analyses for OsBEBT were performed in rice leaves, and SA accumulation/loss is described in leaves.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: bebt leaves showed near-complete loss of SA, and bebt mutants had compromised resistance to M. oryzae.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed methods are in the Results: frameshift mutants, LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling, recombinant enzyme assay, localization, benzylbenzoate complementation, and isotope tracing from labelled BA.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These conditions are all described in the experiments: normal seedling leaves, exogenous BA/benzylbenzoate feeding, and M. oryzae infection.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods explicitly state pEARLY100:35S-OsBEBT/related constructs were used for transient expression/localization assays in N. benthamiana.

Omics_Data.reason: ATTED-II co-expression analysis is explicitly described, but the claim about Extended Data Fig. 10 and NCBI SRA BioProjects with unspecified accessions is not verifiable from the provided text; the figure numbering also appears inconsistent in the excerpt.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: Each listed point matches the Results: co-expression identification, in vitro benzylbenzoate-forming activity, SA loss plus benzoyl-CoA accumulation and benzylbenzoate loss in bebt, rescue by benzylbenzoate, and reduced blast resistance.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly argues against BA as the direct precursor and discusses conservation/homologues in other species including Arabidopsis, tobacco, tomato, wheat and cotton.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The Discussion states there are no obvious growth defects in bebt, bbh or bse mutants.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The Discussion explicitly highlights the specificity of the module, lack of obvious growth defects, and potential to guide breeding for disease resistance.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The Results identify Os09g0441400 as the candidate CYP and later rename it OsBBH.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper explicitly renames Os09g0441400 as benzylbenzoate hydroxylase (OsBBH).

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: OsBBH is described as a CYP and as containing an N-terminal signal peptide for the endomembrane system.

Source_Species.reason: OsBBH is a rice gene characterized in this study.

Source_Species_Latin_Name.reason: Rice is identified as Oryza sativa.

Source_Species_Variety.reason: bbh mutants are stated to be in the ZH11 background.

Applied_Species.reason: Core mutant analyses were performed in rice.

Applied_Species_Latin_Name.reason: The in planta core assays are in Oryza sativa.

Applied_Species_Variety.reason: The rice mutant background for bbh is ZH11.

Biosynthetic_Pathway.reason: OsBBH is identified as the CYP hydroxylase in phenylalanine-derived SA biosynthesis.

Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway.reason: OsBBH is one component of the dedicated terminal BEBT-BBH-BSE SA module.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: Mutant accumulation/rescue data place OsBBH downstream of benzylbenzoate and upstream of BSE-mediated benzylsalicylate hydrolysis.

Primary_Substrate.reason: The paper concludes benzylbenzoate, rather than BA, is the direct hydroxylation substrate of OsBBH.

Primary_Product.reason: Transient and microsomal assays show OsBBH generates benzylsalicylate from benzylbenzoate.

Catalyzed_Reaction_Description.reason: Results and Methods describe NADPH-dependent hydroxylation of benzylbenzoate to benzylsalicylate by OsBBH-containing microsomes.

End_Product_Connection_Type.reason: Benzylsalicylate is shown to be the immediate precursor to SA in the pathway.

Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step.reason: OsBBH is clearly essential and key, but the paper does not explicitly call it rate-limiting.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: OsBBH-YFP co-localized with an ER marker, and microsomal assays support ER association.

Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species.reason: OsBBH was identified as the most strongly OsCNL1-coexpressed CYP, and the paper states the module has pathogen-induced gene expression patterns.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper states the module shows pathogen-induced expression and coordinated regulation with OsCNL1.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: OsBBH is directly linked to SA biosynthesis.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: SA is described as a plant hormone; calling it a phenolic acid is chemically appropriate and consistent with salicylic acid.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The introduction explicitly states SA functions in immunity, abiotic stress response, development and root microbiome assembly.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The OsBBH mutant metabolite analyses were performed in leaves, where SA deficiency was observed.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: bbh mutants showed near-complete loss of SA, benzylbenzoate accumulation, and nearly undetectable benzylsalicylate.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: All listed approaches are in the Results: frameshift mutants, metabolite profiling, transient expression with substrate feeding, GC-MS, microsomal CYP assay, localization, and benzylsalicylate complementation.

Environment_or_Treatment_Factor.reason: These treatments and contexts are explicitly described for OsBBH experiments.

Applied_Promoters.reason: Methods explicitly mention pEARLY100:35S-OsBBH and 35S-mVenus constructs for transient assays.

Omics_Data.reason: ATTED-II co-expression is explicitly supported, but the statement about Extended Data Fig. 10/NCBI SRA BioProjects with unspecified accessions is not directly verifiable from the provided text.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: All five summary points are directly supported by the Results text and Fig. 3/Extended Data Fig. 6.

Other_Important_Info.reason: The paper explicitly says this resolves the longstanding search for the CYP and refutes the BA 2-hydroxylase model in rice.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: The Discussion states no obvious growth defects in bbh mutants.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The Discussion argues these specific enzymes may be better engineering targets than PAL/AIM1/ICS because of specificity and lack of obvious growth defects.

Gene_Accession_Number.reason: The Results identify Os05g0410200 and state it was subsequently renamed OsBSE.

Enzyme_Name.reason: The paper explicitly names Os05g0410200 benzylsalicylate esterase (OsBSE).

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: OsBSE is described as a member of the carboxylesterase (CXE) family.

Metabolic_Step_Position.reason: The Results explicitly state OsBSE catalyses the final step of SA biosynthesis in the module.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: The introduction explicitly lists SA roles in immunity, abiotic stress, development and root microbiome assembly.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The core mutant metabolite analyses for OsBSE were conducted in rice leaves.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: bse mutants showed near-complete SA loss and accumulation of benzylsalicylate and benzylbenzoate.
